title: Modular wedge localization, Majorana fields and the Tsirelson limit of the Bell-CHSH inequality
authors: J. G. A. Caribé, M. S. Guimaraes, I. Roditi, S. P. Sorella
abstract: The massive Majorana field in $1+1$ dimension is employed to investigate the violation of the Bell-CHSH inequality in relativistic Quantum Field Theory. We give an explicit rapidity-space realization of the Summers-Werner modular-localization construction and reduce the vacuum Bell-CHSH correlator to a single spectral weight $h^2(ω)$ for the modular operator. The resulting analytic families approach the Tsirelson bound in the vacuum state as their spectral weight concentrates near $ω\approx0$, corresponding to the eigenvalue $λ^2 \approx 1$ of the modular operator.
summary: This paper explores how the Bell-CHSH inequality is violated in the vacuum state of a 1+1 dimensional massive Majorana field. It shows that the maximum violation, the Tsirelson bound, is a supremum reached when the spectral weight of the modular operator concentrates near zero. The work provides a concrete realization of how modular localization relates to quantum non-locality.
main_problem: Investigating the violation of the Bell-CHSH inequality within the framework of relativistic Quantum Field Theory and determining how the spectral properties of the modular operator influence the approach to the Tsirelson bound.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that the Bell-CHSH correlator in the vacuum state can approach the Tsirelson bound of 2√2 as the spectral weight of the modular operator concentrates near zero, linking the maximum violation to the Type III_1 nature of local algebras.
method: The study utilizes Tomita-Takesaki theory, modular localization, and the Bisognano-Wichmann theorem, implementing the Summers-Werner construction directly in rapidity space.
model_or_system: A massive Majorana field in 1+1 dimensions, utilizing wedge regions and their causal complements in Minkowski spacetime.
important_parameters: Mass (m), rapidity (θ), modular operator eigenvalue (λ), and the spectral weight (h^2(ω))
main_assumptions: Requires the Majorana field to exhibit bounded analytic extension in a specific strip and uses a fermion-parity twist to ensure commuting Bell observables.
figures_summary: Figure 1 is a 3D surface plot showing the Bell-CHSH correlator as a function of Gaussian parameters, identifying the classical and violation regions. Figure 2 shows the correlator behavior as a function of parameter b, demonstrating the approach to the Tsirelson value as b approaches 0.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from the quantization of the Majorana field to the definition of the 1-particle Hilbert space, followed by the application of modular theory to wedge regions, the derivation of a spectral formula for the Bell-CHSH correlator, and finally an analysis of the limit approaching the Tsirelson bound.
why_interesting: This paper provides a deep connection between the algebraic structure of relativistic QFT (Type III von Neumann algebras) and the fundamental limits of quantum correlations (Tsirelson bound), which is highly relevant for understanding the foundations of quantum information in relativistic settings.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: Behavior of the Bell-CHSH correlator ⟨0∣C∣0⟩as a function of the parameters (a,b). The blue surface denotes the classical bound: 2. The orange surface above the blue one corresponds to the values of (a,b) for which violations of the Bell-CHSH inequality take place. Near-Tsirelson values occur for small values of b and a ≈0, where the spectral weight is concentrated near ω = 0.
FIG. 2: Behavior of the Bell-CHSH correlator ⟨0∣C∣0⟩as a function of the parameter b. Large violations take place for small values of b, with the Tsirelson value approached as b →0.
